mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate borrowing costs and compresses equity discount rates, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack suppresses wage inflation expectations and prolongs accommodative policy, generating positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns materialize in less than 50% of triggered months, or the signal yields a negative cumulative alpha over a 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms synchronized consumer and capital expenditure growth, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within the following trading week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of months following industrial production declines, or the signal produces a positive Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 2.8 percent trigger immediate Federal Reserve tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which precedes earnings revisions and positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth rates above 2.0 percent provide a fundamental floor for corporate revenue growth, systematically biasing S&P 500 forward returns positive over the following month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of quarters following GDP growth above 2.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after 2015.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 95 indicates severe capacity underutilization, prompting defensive market positioning and negative short-term returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns fail to materialize in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the threshold produces no statistically significant return differential.
